content.problemStatement:
Recently, Chef has noticed that he is becoming fatter than ever. It is not surprising, as Chef is eating a lot of the food he is cooking. Therefore, he has been doing exercises for the last NNN days (numbered 111 through NNN). For some of these days, Chef remembers for how many minutes he exercised. You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each valid iii, if Ai≥0A_i \ge 0Ai​≥0, then Chef exercised for AiA_iAi​ minutes on the iii-th day; otherwise, Chef forgot how long he exercised on the iii-th day and Ai=−1A_i = -1Ai​=−1. Note that Chef could only have exercised for an integer non-negative number of minutes on each day, including the days he does not remember.

Chef can also compare how long he exercised on each two consecutive days. You are given a string SSS with length N−1N-1N−1. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of SSS is one of the following:


'>' if Chef exercised for strictly more minutes on the iii-th day than on the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)-th day
'=' if Chef exercised for an equally long time on the iii-th day and on the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)-th day
'<' if Chef exercised for strictly more minutes on the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)-th day than on the iii-th day
Chef is doubting his memory, so he wants you to check if the information is consistent, i.e. if there is a way to determine for how many minutes Chef exercised on each day he does not remember such that the string SSS describes the correct comparison operators.
constraints: for each valid iii, if Ai≠−1A_i \neq -1Ai​=−1 and Ai+1≠−1A_{i+1} \neq -1Ai+1​=−1, then the iii-th character of SSS is consistent with AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if the information on the input is inconsistent, print a single line containing the string `"NO"`, otherwise output  `"YES"`

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The number of minutes Chef spent exercising on day 555 must be bigger than 101010 and smaller than 555, which is impossible.
Example case 3: Chef must have exercised for a negative number of minutes on day 222, which is not allowed.
Example case 5: The information is inconsistent because Chef only exercises for an integer number of minutes on each day. Since the number of minutes he spent exercising on each day must be increasing, he must have spent at least 666 minutes on day 222, at least 777 minutes on day 333, at least 888 on day 444 and at least 999 on day 555, but A5=8A_5 = 8A5​=8.
